raw_text: aisling gallagher mob: +44 7446379802 email: aislingg34a@gmail.com core competencies: a highly motivated aerospace engineering graduate, focused on systems integration, actively seeking specialised industry-focused masters opportunities. hands-on experience in satellite systems, thermal control and flight dynamics modelling. proven ability to apply technical expertise to spacecraft/aircraft design, subsystem integration and performance analysis. very strong leadership and groupwork skills, nurtured through leading roles in large industry-led project experience and practise pitching design concepts and research to engineers and academics. skilled in organisation, time management and professional practise, with a strategic mindset and calm, solutions-based approach in high pressure scenarios. brings a systems-level perspective to collaborative problem solving, with particular interest in analysing technical interfaces and aligning detailed engineering with wider mission requirements. a clear communicator and thoughtful decision maker, who thrives in dynamic, high-performance environments, bringing initiative, adaptability and emotional intelligence to inter-discipline collaboration. education university of bristol - meng (master of engineering) in aerospace engineering 2021 - 2025 o awarded first class honours. sacred heart grammar school, newry 2014 - 2021 o alevels: a*, a*, a maths, physics, chemistry o gcses: 10 a*. engineering internships manna drones, ireland june 2022 august 2022 o full time summer internship at an exciting drone-delivery start-up in dublin, rotating across engineering, manufacturing, operation and test flight teams. o led a cassette optimisation project: designed, prototyped and implemented improvements to the drones electronics and payload housing support created official manufacturing documentation for future builds. o conducted comparative testing and analysis of battery options under the guidance of the lead power engineer evaluating performance, safety, and lifecycle data, and authored this section of the technical report for the easa approval of mannas next-gen drone. o practised cad (solidworks) skills, designing and 3d printing a component for the spooling machine, enhancing the functionality of the delivery drop mechanism. o hands-on experience at the test flight site, learning about the flight validation and verification procedures. learned how to fly the drones and completed some of the test flights for scenario-based testing, contributing to the flight-hour accumulation and performance approval. technical skills o modelling and simulation software: solidworks, autodesk fusion360, gmat, msc patran/nastran, openrocket, nasa gmat, simulink, xflr5, xfoil, ashes. o programming & scripting: python and matlab. o documentation and productivity: microsoft office suite (word, excel, powerpoint), latex (overleaf). academic projects and interests 2024: airbus space and defence project: satellite for space weather monitoring o collaborated with airbus space and defence to design a satellite for space weather monitoring and cme detection at lagrange point 5, accommodating a suite of cme detection instruments, as inspired by the esa vigil mission. systems engineer: o developed full system architecture and high-level operational definitions led interface management for compliance with airbus distributed requirement specification, managing critical subsystem interdependencies. conducted thorough risk analysis procedures and implemented design mitigations. presented final work to a team of vigil mission engineers. thermal control engineer: o developed a robust thermal control system (active/passive components) that reliably accommodates and maintains all design components/payloads within their operational limits. role included modelling the varying thermal environments, material analysis and selection of internal and external coatings/ mli design. 2023/2024: individual research project: loss-of-control bifurcation analysis of nasas gtt configuration. o conducted a bifurcation analysis on nasas generic t-tailed transport model to investigate the onset of deep stall, using the 4" and 8" order models and auto computational solver software. o simulated equilibrium solutions and time history responses to investigate the dynamic outputs at different control surface set-ups and stability conditions to enhance the understanding of the t-tails effect on control and stability in flight. o designed and simulated recovery routes using control surface inputs, whilst simultaneously analysing stability. simulated the onset of an unrecoverable deep stall and deduced interesting insights about the conditions which induce it. o delivered a formal presentation of my research to a panel of academics in the field. o 2023/2024: aircraft vehicle design and systems integration project 3 o systems engineer: systems engineering for a uav for visual inspection of electronic grids, designed functional breakdown and system architecture, quantified reliability and risk metrics, conducted critical analysis of requirements and derived subsystem level requirements and trade-off studies for interface management. o rotorcraft performance engineer: used an industry-standard leonardo helicopters tool to design and size a medium sized penny-farthing configuration, by conducting thorough analysis of performance metrics, such as payload capacity, fuel efficiency, blade sizing and power requirements. o aerodynamics engineer: conducted aerodynamic optimisation for a single-aisle, twin engine airliner, using xflrs. assessed drag coefficients and breakdowns and optimised wing geometry. 2023/2024 nrc: national rocketry championship. o aerodynamics engineer: contributed to the design of a competition model rocket aimed at 2,500 ft altitude responsible for nose cone and fin design, drag profiling, and motor selection using openrocket project completed with bristol seds (students for the exploration and development of space) society. 2022/2023: uav design, build and test: o assistant team leader: leadership role for a student group designing, building and testing a wing design for a medical supply delivery uav, overseeing project coordination and technical planning. o aerodynamics engineer: performed an aerodynamic analysis and aerofoil down-selection using x-foil software, drag calculations, wind tunnel operation for final validation testing, and data processing. work experience o university of bristol student union: intramural sports assistant september 2024 may 2025 part-time job with university su, organising and leading recreational sports leagues, for 3000+ students. o neilson holiday resorts, italy and greece childrens activity leader june 2024 august 2024 children s activity leader at sports centred beach resorts in sardinia and greece, caring for up to 30 children at a time, leading sports activities including swimming, water sports, team sports and childcare. o private piano teaching business september 2019 - september 2021 planned and taught individual piano lessons for 10+ children and adult beginners, learning how to market a business, organize schedules and provide good customer service to help promote business growth. activities and interests o bristol seds (students for exploration and development of space) 2023/2024, 2024/2025 attending guest lecture events, skills workshops and involvement in the nrc (national rocketry competition) o university of bristol basketball women s team captain 2022/2023, 2023/2024, 2024/2025 competed in weekly games for bucs inter-university basketball leagues for 4 seasons, captaining for 3 seasons. o spartans basketball club committee member 2022/2023, 2023/2024, 2024/2025 helped run a 150+ person student-run society, with a committee role for 3 consecutive years. included hosting large sports events, running internal leagues, admin for 5 competitive teams, managing club finances, organising coaching staff and referees and collaborating with the universitys students union. o basketball coaching - be:active volunteer 2023-2025 volunteer student coach for su beginner basketball initiative, for 2 consecutive years, coaching up to 40 players weekly. o university irish society committee member 2024-2025 organising and hosting cultural, sports, music, language and social events for irish students in bristol. complete uk driving license obtained. academic and other references are available upon
